trivia_bullets.text: Ogre Errant has the creature type Ogre Knight — unusual in Eldraine where ogres are Wilds creatures, not court members. The flavor text's punchline explains the contradiction without granting genuine knighthood: the courts retroactively designated an unconquerable ogre as an ally.
"Knights kept returning from Ogre's Pass wearing nothing but bruises, so they decided to just pretend it was on their side." — No named speaker. Military failure reframed as diplomatic pragmatism.

Triggered ability grants menace to another attacking Knight until end of turn. Understatted at 3/4 for four mana at common for competitive play, but functions as a limited role-player in knight-dense formats.

Illustrated by Mitchell Malloy in a brooding portrait register — a weary wanderer, not a raging brute. 'Errant' echoes Arthurian knight-errant tradition: a roving warrior without fixed allegiance or lord.

related_cards.name: Fervent Champion
related_cards.note: Knight that Ogre Errant's ability can grant menace to; same set

related_cards.name: Brimstone Trebuchet
related_cards.note: Shares the unattached-military-unit flavor within ELD's red commons
